target.name: RobCo
target.founder: Roman Hölzl, Paul Maroldt, Constantin Dresel

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $160M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $2.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

private.name: Bright Machines
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C)

data.narrative:
RobCo is a Series C robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $160M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of nine private and one public company operating in the same sector at similar or adjacent stages, including peers such as Hadrian, Nimble Robotics, Exotec, and Dexterity.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy for current enterprise value, applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, yielding $272M. Peer-median analysis across five comparable-stage companies—Hadrian and Nimble Robotics (both Series C at $1.6B and $1.0B respectively), Exotec (Series D, $2.0B), Bedrock Robotics (Series B, $1.8B), and Dexterity (Series B, $1.6B)—establishes a median valuation of $1.6B with a range of $1.0B to $2.0B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $231M (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median of $961M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty regarding RobCo's relative positioning within the peer set. Key variables driving valuation include the company's revenue run-rate and growth trajectory relative to peers, technology differentiation, and customer concentration—none of which are addressed in available data. Stage progression and peer comparables diverge significantly, suggesting the peer set may reflect either more mature or better-capitalized competitors.
